name: Hawaiian Mission Houses
tagline: The oldest surviving Western buildings in Hawaii (1821) — honest, nuanced storytelling about missionaries' complex legacy.
parking: Metered street parking on King St
bestTime: Guided tour times vary; Wednesday evening events are popular
talkStory: The oldest surviving Western-style buildings in Hawaii (dating to 1821) sit in the shadow of downtown Honolulu's skyscrapers. Guided tours reveal the complex story of the missionaries who arrived in Hawaii and their lasting impact on Hawaiian culture, for better and worse. The printing house where Hawaiian-language texts were first produced is particularly fascinating. Locals appreciate the nuanced, honest storytelling.
